meta.name: ESS Epworth Sleepiness Scale
meta.description: The ESS is an 8-item self-report questionnaire that measures general level of daytime sleepiness. Respondents rate the likelihood of dozing off in 8 common situations, widely used to screen for sleep disorders.
meta.author: Johns MW (1991)
meta.reference: Johns MW. A new method for measuring daytime sleepiness: the Epworth sleepiness scale. Sleep. 1991;14(6):540-545.
meta.instruction: How likely are you to doze off or fall asleep in the following situations, in contrast to feeling just tired? This refers to your usual way of life in recent times. Even if you have not done some of these things recently, try to work out how they would have affected you.

questions.text: Sitting, inactive in a public place (e.g., a theater or a meeting)
As a passenger in a car for an hour without a break
Lying down to rest in the afternoon when circumstances permit
Sitting quietly after a lunch without alcohol
In a car, while stopped for a few minutes in traffic

ranges.label: Lower Normal Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness level is within the normal range, indicating adequate sleep quality and duration. You appear to maintain good alertness throughout typical daily activities.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current sleep hygiene habits and consistent sleep-wake schedule
Stay physically active during the day to support restorative sleep quality at night
Avoid excessive caffeine intake in the afternoon and evening hours

ranges.label: Higher Normal Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is at the higher end of the normal range. While not clinically significant, this level suggests room for improvement in sleep habits or overall sleep quality.
suggestions: Ensure you are consistently getting 7-9 hours of quality sleep each night
Limit exposure to electronic screens at least one hour before bedtime to support natural melatonin production
Evaluate whether stress, irregular work schedules, or lifestyle factors may be affecting your sleep quality
Consult a healthcare provider if daytime sleepiness begins to interfere with your daily performance or safety

ranges.label: Mild Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is mildly elevated above the normal range. This may indicate insufficient sleep duration, poor sleep quality, or an underlying sleep disturbance that warrants attention.
suggestions: Keep a detailed sleep diary for at least two weeks to identify patterns in your sleep quality and duration
Evaluate and reduce factors that may impair sleep such as caffeine, alcohol, heavy meals before bed, and irregular schedules
Create an optimal sleep environment by ensuring your bedroom is dark, quiet, cool, and free from electronic distractions
Consider consulting a sleep medicine specialist if sleepiness persists despite improving sleep habits
Avoid napping for more than 20 minutes during the day as prolonged naps can disrupt nighttime sleep architecture

ranges.label: Moderate Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is moderately elevated, suggesting a possible underlying sleep disorder. Medical evaluation is recommended as this level of sleepiness may impair alertness during critical activities.
suggestions: Schedule an appointment with a sleep medicine specialist for a thorough clinical evaluation
A polysomnography or home sleep apnea test may be recommended to assess for conditions like obstructive sleep apnea
Exercise caution when driving or operating machinery, as your level of sleepiness may impair reaction time and judgment
Discuss with your physician whether any current medications may be contributing to your excessive daytime sleepiness

ranges.label: Severe Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is severely elevated, strongly suggesting an underlying sleep disorder such as sleep apnea or narcolepsy. Prompt medical evaluation is essential for your health and safety.
suggestions: Seek urgent evaluation from a sleep medicine specialist to identify the cause of your severe daytime sleepiness
Undergo a comprehensive sleep study including polysomnography and potentially a multiple sleep latency test
Avoid driving, operating heavy machinery, or performing safety-critical tasks until you have been properly diagnosed and treated
Inform household members and close contacts about your condition so they can provide support and monitor for safety concerns
Ask your doctor about interim management strategies while awaiting definitive diagnosis and treatment
